Class CookingStationService

java.lang.Object
net.sylphian.minecraft.cooking.station.CookingStationService
public class CookingStationService extends Object
Manages the lifecycle of all active cooking stations.

Stations are loaded from PDC on first interaction and held in an in-memory map for the duration of their activity. The service runs a tick loop every 20 game ticks (once per second) that advances cook progress and consumes fuel for any station that has a matching recipe and remaining fuel.

When a station becomes fully idle (no ingredients, no fuel, no output, no viewers) it is evicted from memory and its PDC is cleared.

  • Constructor Details

    • CookingStationService

      public CookingStationService(org.bukkit.plugin.java.JavaPlugin plugin, List<CookingRecipe> recipes, Map<org.bukkit.Material, Integer> fuels, CookingStationGui gui)
      Constructs a new CookingStationService.
      Parameters:
      plugin - the owning plugin
      recipes - all loaded cooking recipes
      fuels - map of fuel material → burn time in ticks
      gui - the GUI factory used to open and update station inventories

  • Method Details

    • start

      public void start()
      Starts the background tick loop. Call from JavaPlugin.onEnable().

    • shutdown

      public void shutdown()
      Stops the tick loop and persists all active station states to PDC. Call from JavaPlugin.onDisable().

    • openStation

      public void openStation(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, org.bukkit.block.Block block)
      Opens the cooking station GUI for the given player at the given block. Loads PDC state if this is the first time the station is being accessed.
      Parameters:
      player - the player opening the station
      block - the furnace or campfire block

    • syncFromInventory

      public void syncFromInventory(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, org.bukkit.inventory.Inventory inv)
      Flushes the current contents of an open GUI inventory directly into the in-memory station state. Call this before closeStation(Player) to guarantee that any items moved in the same tick as the close are captured before the PDC write.

      This exists because deferred 1-tick syncs scheduled during click handling race against InventoryCloseEvent: the close fires on the same tick as the click, so a pending sync task may not have run yet when the state is persisted.

      Parameters:
      player - the player closing the station
      inv - the station's GUI inventory at close time

    • closeStation

      public void closeStation(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Called when a player closes their cooking station GUI. Saves state to PDC and evicts the station from memory if it is now idle.
      Parameters:
      player - the player who closed the GUI

    • getOpenStationLocation

      public org.bukkit.Location getOpenStationLocation(UUID uuid)
      Returns the location of the station currently open for the given player, or null if they have no station open.

    • getState

      public CookingStationState getState(org.bukkit.Location location)
      Returns the in-memory state of the station at the given location, or null.

    • destroyStation

      public void destroyStation(org.bukkit.block.Block block)
      Handles the destruction of a cooking station block. Closes any open GUIs, drops stored items at the block's location, removes in-memory state, and clears PDC.
      Parameters:
      block - the block being destroyed

    • setIngredient

      public void setIngredient(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, int ingredientIdx, org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item)
      Applies a change to an ingredient slot of the station currently open for the player.
      Parameters:
      player - the player interacting
      ingredientIdx - ingredient slot index (0–4)
      item - the new item (null to clear)

    • setFuel

      public void setFuel(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item)
      Applies a change to the fuel slot of the station currently open for the player.

    • clearOutput

      public void clearOutput(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Clears the output slot of the station currently open for the player.

    • isValidFuel

      public boolean isValidFuel(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item)
      Returns true if the given item is a recognised fuel.

    • reload

      public void reload(List<CookingRecipe> newRecipes, Map<org.bukkit.Material, Integer> newFuels)
      Replaces the recipe and fuel lists used for future ticks.
      Parameters:
      newRecipes - updated recipe list
      newFuels - updated fuel map
